    - A committed live-probe artifact proves the auth signal is FREE — `pi
      --list-models` is already auth-filtered (removing GEMINI_API_KEY makes all

      (ROADMAP SC-5, D-09).
    - "`doctor` and `plan` advertise only channels pi can genuinely serve:
      `discoverChannels` intersects the operator's config seed with pi's
      servable set, so a config-seeded channel pi holds no credential for is
      never advertised (ROADMAP SC-4, D-11)."
    - An operator pin to an unservable pi model is rejected BEFORE any worker
      dispatch — `resolvePin` already throws RoutingError for a channel absent
      from the discovered list; feeding it truthful channels is the whole fix
      (ROADMAP SC-4). `src/route/router.ts` needs NO edit.
    - "An INCONCLUSIVE probe fails closed on the CHANNEL ONLY, never on the run:
      `servable === undefined` means UNKNOWN ⇒ no filtering ⇒ pi's channels are
      advertised exactly as today. `[]` means 'serves nothing'; `undefined`
      means 'we do not know' — a failed/timed-out `pi --list-models` must yield
      `undefined`, never `[]` (ROADMAP SC-4, D-10/D-12)."
    - "Blast radius is zero for the other four adapters: `servable` is
      additive-optional and only pi sets it, so
      claude-code/codex/cursor-agent/opencode channel sets are byte-identical,
      `DEFAULT_CONFIG`'s pi seed (`zai/glm-5.2`) survives, and `pickReviewer`
      still resolves pi (D-12)."
    - The probe artifact records PROVIDER NAMES AND STRING LENGTHS ONLY —
      `~/.pi/agent/auth.json` holds live oauth tokens and API keys; no
      credential value is ever transcribed into git.

Close HYG-05: `doctor` and `plan` never advertise a pi channel pi cannot actually serve, and an operator pin to one is rejected before any worker is dispatched.
